message.content: Search for GPU cloud computing options that offer: (1) university/academic/student discount programs or research credit programs, (2) flat monthly subscription pricing (not per-hour). Look specifically at: Google Colab, Paperspace/Gradient, CoreWeave, AWS/GCP/Azure academic programs, NVIDIA LaunchPad, NSF ACCESS (formerly XSEDE), Kaggle, Lightning.ai, Modal.com, Replicate. Also check if Vast.ai or RunPod have any academic programs. Return what you find including pricing and eligibility requirements.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Google Colab Pro academic and student pricing for 2026:

## Free Colab Pro for Students and Faculty

Google is offering a limited number of completely free, 1-year Colab Pro subscriptions to faculty and students at U.S.-based higher education institutions, and these free subscriptions are available to anyone who can verify their identity as a faculty member or student at a U.S.-based higher education institution.

This initiative is designed to provide greater access to enhanced computational resources, allowing for more complex data analysis, faster model training, and an overall smoother experience in your data science coursework.

## How to Sign Up

You can click a "No cost for students and educators" button to be redirected to SheerID, Colab's third party identity verification partner, to verify your eligibility, and SheerID will guide you through the process of verifying your eligibility.

## Regular Pricing

For those not eligible for the free academic offer, Colab Pro costs $11.99/mo, and Pro+ costs $49.99/mo with better GPUs.

Note that if the link for free education subscriptions is not present, it means that discounted Colab Pro for Education subscriptions have run out for the time being.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Paperspace Gradient and academic research programs:

## Paperspace Gradient Overview

Paperspace Gradient offers a free tier with limited GPU hours for notebooks and ML workflows. Paperspace Gradient is beginner-friendly with its free tier and one-click signup, ideal for learning MLOps.

## Academic Research Program

Paperspace offers a structured research program called the **Advanced Technologies Group (ATG) Research Fellowship**. The Research Fellowship is a 10-15 week paid program that is designed to bring in Graduate and post-Graduate students from a wide variety of disciplines who want to apply their passion for research in a practical setting.

The ATG Research Fellowship bridges this gap, engaging Paperspace engineers with some of the brightest minds in ML/AI research. The ATG Research Fellowship is a structured, collaborative environment in which the company and core products benefit from the cutting edge insights of academia.

Researchers-in-residence gain practical experience, but retain total autonomy to pursue their interests, while making the most of Paperspace's products and expertise.

The search results indicate that while Paperspace Gradient does offer free tiers for general use, the primary academic research program is the paid ATG Research Fellowship rather than a dedicated academic credit program. For larger research projects, many cloud providers (AWS, Google Cloud, Azure) offer academic credits to students through university partnerships or specific programs.

## NVIDIA Academic Grant Program

The Academic Grant Program advances academic research by providing world-class computing access and resources to researchers. It is now accepting proposals for academic research projects in a variety of topic areas.

### Research Areas and Available Resources

Areas of interest include scientific simulation, quantum computing, and physics-informed machine learning. Resources available include up to 30,000 NVIDIA H100 80 GB hours or up to eight NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 GPUs.

### 2026 Timeline

The deadline for proposal submission is June 30, with award decisions expected in September. I've completed the web search for "AWS GCP Azure academic GPU credits research program 2026". Here are the key findings:

## Academic GPU Credits Programs

### Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
PhD students may apply once a year for credits of up to $1,000 USD, while faculty and postdoctoral researchers are eligible for one award of up to $5,000 USD. Credit awards expire 365 days from the credit issuance date, or when the credit amount has been fully used.

### AWS Cloud Credit for Research
The AWS Cloud Credit for Research program provides AWS Promotional Credit to researchers that are using technology to accelerate innovation. Student awards will be up to a maximum of $5,000.00, while faculty and staff awards are not capped. Effective February 16, 2026, Free Tier accounts will be ineligible for promotional credits.

### Azure for Students
In 2026, major cloud providers offering free GPU credits include Microsoft Azure ($200 for new users), and Azure provides Azure cloud credits for education.

### General Free Resources
Free GPU trials and credits provide valuable resources in 2026: Google Cloud's $300, AWS Activate's startup credits, Azure's $200, educational programs offering $100-500, and unlimited access through Colab and Kaggle.

These programs are designed to support academic research and enable students and faculty to access substantial GPU computing resources for their projects.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about NSF ACCESS (formerly XSEDE) GPU computing research credits and 2026 eligibility:

## Program Overview
ACCESS (Advanced Cyberinfrastructure Coordination Ecosystem: Services & Support), formerly XSEDE, is a National Science Foundation-funded, nationwide collection of supercomputing systems available to researchers through merit-based allocations. XSEDE concluded formal operations as a National Science Foundation (NSF) funded project on August 31, 2022. Similar services are now operated through NSF's follow-on program, Advanced Cyberinfrastructure Coordination Ecosystem: Services & Support, or ACCESS.

## GPU Computing Resources
Compute resources in the ACCESS portfolio include high-performance computing clusters (GPU and CPU based) accessed by logging in to front-end nodes.

## Eligibility
U.S. researchers, educators, students, and international collaborators are eligible to use ACCESS resources, adhering to NSF and ACCESS policies. Additionally, resources and services provided through ACCESS are available at no cost, and funding is generally not a criteria for the use of ACCESS.

## Allocation Types and Credits
Allocations are awarded in the form of ACCESS Credits for the first three levels. With your ACCESS ID, researchers can apply for an allocation and receive credits which can be exchanged for access to one or more resources.

## 2026 Allocation Deadlines
For researchers with the largest-scale computational, analysis, and storage needs, requests for Maximize ACCESS allocations can be submitted during the period of December 15, 2025 to January 31, 2026, with awards starting April 1, 2026. The following proposal submission window will be June 15, 2026 to July 31, 2026, with awards starting October 1, 2026.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Modal.com pricing and academic research credits:

## Academic Research Credits

Startups and academic researchers can get up to $25k free compute credits on Modal. Additionally, Startups and researchers can also apply for up to $50K in free credits.

## General Pricing Structure

Modal uses a usage-based pricing model:

- New customers get $30 / month free compute credit
- Pricing for GPUs varies based on the type of GPU, with rates starting at $0.000164 per second for Nvidia T4 GPUs and going up to $0.001097 per second for Nvidia H100 GPUs
- Costs for CPU usage start at $0.0000131 per core per second
- Memory is charged at $0.00000222 per GiB per second

## Plan Options

Starter Plan: Free for up to $30/month in credits, Team Plan: Priced at $250/month, and Enterprise Plan: Custom pricing for larger organizations

The academic program appears to be available through Modal's academics application process on their website.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Kaggle GPU compute limits for the free tier in 2026:

## GPU Compute Limits for Kaggle Free Tier (2026)

As of 2026, Kaggle offers a weekly quota, often around 30 hours of GPU time. More specifically, Kaggle provides 30 hours per week of free GPU time (P100 GPUs with 16GB memory) for running competitions and notebooks.

### Additional Features:

- **Session Duration**: Sessions can run for up to 9 hours, and critically, they often support background execution. This means your training can continue even if you close the browser tab – a massive advantage for longer runs.

- **Storage**: You get 20 GB of persistent storage, which is quite helpful for storing datasets or model checkpoints across sessions.

- **Public by Default**: Notebooks are public by default on the free tier. If you're working on something sensitive or proprietary, you'll need to upgrade or be mindful of what you share.

The free tier remains completely accessible without requiring a credit card or payment, making Kaggle a solid option for students and hobbyists looking to experiment with GPU-accelerated machine learning.

What are the four allocation tiers (Explore, Discover, Accelerate, Maximize)? What are the credit amounts, who is eligible (students vs faculty), and how long does approval take?

content.content:
# ACCESS Allocation Tiers

Based on the provided documentation, here's what's specified about the four tiers:

## Explore ACCESS
- **Requirements**: Summary of planned work, PI/co-PI CVs (PDF), project details (title, keywords, field of science, grant info)
- **Student eligibility**: Graduate students must submit a signed advisor letter on institutional letterhead confirming awareness and engagement
- **Approval timeline**: Not specified in the document

## Discover ACCESS
- **Requirements**: Activity summary, PI/co-PI CVs, project info, one-page project description (PDF) addressing research objectives and resource needs
- **Student eligibility**: Same advisor letter requirement as Explore
- **Approval timeline**: Not specified in the document

## Accelerate ACCESS
- **Requirements**: Activity summary, PI/co-PI CVs, three-page project description addressing research objectives, resource estimates, computational plan, software needs, and team qualifications
- **Eligibility note**: "Researchers are expected to have reasonably well defined plans"
- **Approval timeline**: Not specified in the document

## Maximize ACCESS
- **Approval timeline**: Semi-annual cycles with specific windows (e.g., December 15, 2025 – January 31, 2026, with awards starting April 1, 2026)
- **Note**: The document does not specify credit amounts, student eligibility restrictions, or detailed requirements for this tier

**Important**: The provided content does not include specific credit amounts for any tier.

# NVIDIA Academic Grant Program: Key Details

## Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must be "a full-time faculty member at an accredited academic institution that awards research degrees to PhD students." Projects must incorporate pretrained models from ai.nvidia.com or extensively use NVIDIA software distributions, with timelines aligned to expected GPU hours or hardware delivery dates.

## Available Resources

The program offers different benefits by research category:

- **Simulation and Modeling**: Up to 30,000 NVIDIA H100 80 GB hours or eight RTX PRO 6000 GPUs
- **Data Science**: Up to 30,000 H100 hours or two DGX Spark supercomputers
- **Robotics and Edge AI**: Up to 30,000 H100 hours, two AGX Developer Kits, and/or four RTX PRO 6000 GPUs

Additional benefits include "letters of support for grant applications" and "opportunities to present at GTC and network through NVIDIA's global channels."

## What NVIDIA Provides

Beyond hardware/computing access, the program offers marketing support consideration, grant application assistance, and presentation opportunities at major NVIDIA events.

## Application Deadlines

The page does not specify explicit submission deadlines for current calls for proposals.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Google Colab Pro free for students with SheerID eligibility verification:

## Eligibility Requirements

Anyone who is a student or faculty member at a US-based university and is located in the US at the time of verification is eligible. Google is offering a limited number of completely free, 1-year Colab Pro subscriptions to faculty and students at U.S.-based higher education institutions.

## Verification Process with SheerID

To sign up, click the "No cost for students and educators" button to be redirected to SheerID, Colab's third party identity verification partner, to verify your eligibility. 

Make sure that you fill out the form with the same email address for the account that you were logged into when you accessed the signup page in Colab (as opposed to your .edu email address) or else your verification will not be recorded.

After submitting the SheerID form, they will notify you whether you were instantly verified or ask for more documentation/information. SheerID will keep in touch with you via email until the process is completed.

## Geographic Restriction

The program is specifically restricted to US-based institutions and US-located individuals at the time of verification—it is indeed US only as you specified in your query.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Here is a comprehensive breakdown of what I found. I've split the results into two categories you care about — academic/research credit programs and flat monthly subscription pricing — and flagged which ones have neither.

---

## Google Colab

**Free Pro for US Students and Faculty**
- 1 year of Colab Pro free (normally $11.99/month) for verified US students and faculty at accredited US higher-education institutions
- Colab Pro+ ($49.99/month) is not included
- Verification is via SheerID (third-party identity verification); you must use the same Google account email you used on the signup page, not your .edu address
- US-only, US-located at time of verification required
- Supply is limited — Google notes subscriptions may run out
- Flat monthly plans: Yes — Pro at $11.99/month, Pro+ at $49.99/month (both are subscriptions, not per-hour)

**Best for:** Students who qualify and are US-based. Lowest friction of any option here.

---

## Lightning.ai

**Academic Tier — 80% off Pro**
- Pro plan is $50/month standard; with the academic tier it drops to approximately $10/month
- Designed for students and researchers needing GPU access
- Verification required (edu email or institutional affiliation)
- Flat monthly subscription: Yes — this is a subscription model, not per-hour billing
- GPU hours are included in the plan; non-GPU tasks run on CPU to preserve the GPU budget

**Best for:** Ongoing ML work on a budget with a flat predictable bill.

---

## NSF ACCESS (formerly XSEDE)

**Free allocation program for US researchers — no cost at all**
- Four tiers: Explore, Discover, Accelerate, Maximize
- Explore/Discover: fastest approval (1–2 business days for Explore), good entry points
- Accelerate/Maximize: require more documentation, semi-annual review cycles; next Maximize window opens June 15–July 31, 2026
- Resources include GPU and CPU HPC clusters at national supercomputing centers
- Graduate students can apply but must include a signed advisor letter on institutional letterhead
- Faculty PIs apply directly; students apply under a PI or with advisor endorsement
- No cost whatsoever — credits are exchanged for compute time, not dollars
- Not a subscription — it is a merit-based allocation, but it is completely free

**Best for:** Academic research with a faculty PI. Most powerful option but requires institutional affiliation and some paperwork.

---

## NVIDIA Academic Grant Program

**Hardware and cloud-hour grants for faculty researchers**
- Eligibility is restricted to full-time faculty at accredited institutions that award PhD degrees — students cannot apply directly
- Resources offered (by research area):
  - Simulation/Modeling: up to 30,000 H100 80GB hours OR 8x RTX PRO 6000 GPUs
  - Data Science: up to 30,000 H100 hours OR 2x DGX Spark supercomputers
  - Robotics/Edge AI: up to 30,000 H100 hours, AGX Dev Kits, and/or RTX PRO 6000 GPUs
- Projects must use pretrained models from ai.nvidia.com or NVIDIA software distributions
- Annual cycle; 2026 deadline was June 30, awards in September
- Not a subscription model — it is a grant of compute hours or physical hardware

**Best for:** Faculty with a well-defined research project. PhD students cannot apply independently.

---

## AWS Cloud Credit for Research

**Grant-style credits, not a subscription**
- Student awards: up to $5,000 in AWS promotional credits
- Faculty/staff awards: no cap stated
- Covers GPU instances (P/G/Trn families) on AWS
- As of February 16, 2026, Free Tier accounts are ineligible for promotional credits — you need a standard account
- Application via aws.amazon.com/grants
- Not flat monthly — credits are spent per-hour as you use resources

**Best for:** Researchers already in the AWS ecosystem who need a burst of compute for a defined project.

---

## Google Cloud Platform (GCP) Research Credits

**Credit grants, not a subscription**
- PhD students: up to $1,000/year
- Faculty and postdoctoral researchers: up to $5,000 (one-time award)
- Credits expire 365 days from issuance or when exhausted
- Apply once per year; reviewed by Google
- Not flat monthly — credits are consumed per-hour

**Best for:** PhD students or faculty doing GCP-native work (TPUs, Vertex AI, etc.).

---

## Azure for Students / Azure Research Credits

- Azure for Students: $100 in credits for students with a verified .edu email, no credit card required, valid 12 months
- Azure also has a separate research credits program for faculty-led projects (amounts vary, applied through institutional channels)
- Not a flat subscription for GPU compute — credits are spent per-hour

**Best for:** Getting started quickly with no credit card if you have a .edu address.

---

## Modal.com

**Academic research credits program**
- Up to $10,000 in credits for researchers (the Academics page specifies this; some secondary sources mention up to $25k–$50k but those appear to be for the broader startup/researcher track)
- Credits are applied toward compute usage (GPU time), not subscription fees
- Grants are tied to conference submission timelines — credits expire once conference results are finalized, and are granted once per conference cycle
- Apply via modal.com/academics; educators contact partnerships@modal.com
- Not a flat monthly subscription — it is a per-second billing model with credits offsetting costs
- Free tier: $30/month in compute credits for all new accounts regardless

**Best for:** Researchers with a paper submission deadline who need a burst of GPU compute. The conference-cycle structure is unusual but can be very generous.

---

## Kaggle

**Completely free, no academic program needed**
- 30 hours/week of free GPU time (NVIDIA T4, 16 GB VRAM) for all verified users
- Sessions up to 9 hours; background execution supported
- 20 GB persistent storage
- No credit card, no application, no academic verification required
- Not a subscription — just a free weekly quota

**Best for:** Prototyping, experimentation, and competition work. The 30-hour/week cap is the main limitation.

---

## Paperspace / Gradient (now DigitalOcean)

- No dedicated academic discount or credit program found. The ATG Research Fellowship is a paid employment program, not a compute credit grant.
- Free tier exists for notebooks but is limited
- Subscription plans available (Growth at $8/month, Pro at $39/month) but no academic pricing
- GPU compute is charged per-hour on top of subscription

**Verdict:** No academic program.

---

## CoreWeave

- No dedicated academic credit program. CoreWeave has a Startup Accelerator that gives cloud credits to venture-backed startups.
- The Chan Zuckerberg Initiative (CZI) separately runs an RFA that provides access to a CoreWeave GPU cluster for biology/AI researchers — worth checking at chanzuckerberg.com/rfa/ai-computing-gpu if your work is in that space.
- Pricing is per-hour, no flat subscription model.

**Verdict:** No direct academic program. CZI RFA is a workaround for biology-adjacent research.

---

## Replicate.com

- No academic credits or student discount program found. Pricing is pay-as-you-go per inference/compute second. Volume discounts exist but are usage-based, not academic.

**Verdict:** No academic program.

---

## Vast.ai

- Has a dedicated education page (vast.ai/education) aimed at universities and research institutions, but the details require direct contact with their team — no publicly listed credit amounts or eligibility criteria were findable.
- Also has a Startup Program ($2,500 in credits) but that is for VC-backed startups.
- Pricing is marketplace-style (per-hour spot pricing), not flat monthly.

**Verdict:** Possible education program exists but terms are not publicly disclosed — worth a direct inquiry.

---

## RunPod

- No formal academic program. Student discounts may be verifiable through UNiDAYS or SheerID but nothing is officially listed.
- Has a Startup Program for VC-backed startups, not students.
- Pricing is per-hour spot/on-demand, not flat monthly.

**Verdict:** No academic program.

---

## Summary Table

| Platform | Academic/Student Program | Flat Monthly Option | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Google Colab | Yes — free Pro for 1 year | Yes ($11.99/mo Pro) | US-only, limited supply |
| Lightning.ai | Yes — 80% off (~$10/mo) | Yes | Best flat-rate academic deal |
| NSF ACCESS | Yes — fully free allocations | N/A (free) | Faculty PI usually required |
| NVIDIA Academic Grant | Yes — H100 hours or hardware | No | PhD-granting faculty only |
| AWS Research Credits | Yes — up to $5K (students) | No (per-hour) | Good for AWS-native work |
| GCP Research Credits | Yes — up to $1K PhD / $5K faculty | No (per-hour) | Annual application |
| Azure for Students | Yes — $100 credits | No (per-hour) | Easiest to get, lowest amount |
| Modal.com | Yes — up to $10K | No (per-second) | Tied to conference cycles |
| Kaggle | N/A — free for everyone | N/A (free) | 30 hrs/week T4, no application |
| Paperspace/Gradient | No | No academic pricing | No program |
| CoreWeave | No (CZI RFA is adjacent) | No | No direct program |
| Replicate | No | No | No program |
| Vast.ai | Possibly — details not public | No | Contact them directly |
| RunPod | No | No | No program |

For the ZH Copilot project specifically, the two most practical options are: **Lightning.ai at ~$10/month** (flat, predictable, works immediately) and **NSF ACCESS** (free but requires institutional affiliation and a faculty advisor if you are a student).

Sources:
- [Colab for higher education: Free Colab Pro for students and more](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/education/colab-higher-education/)
- [Jeff Dean on X — Colab Pro free for verified US students](https://x.com/JeffDean/status/1946437195121963777)
- [Colab Paid Services Pricing](https://colab.research.google.com/signup)
- [Lightning AI Students](https://lightning.ai/docs/team-management/academia/students)
- [Lightning AI on X — academic tier pricing](https://x.com/LightningAI/status/2001293100539412587)
- [Meet Lightning AI's Academic tier (LinkedIn)](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/pytorch-lightning_meet-lightning-ais-academic-tier-all-activity-7367295996277514240-o403)
- [NSF ACCESS — For Researchers](https://access-ci.org/get-started/for-researchers/)
- [NSF ACCESS — For Graduate Students](https://access-ci.org/get-started/for-graduate-students/)
- [ACCESS Allocations — Prepare Requests](https://allocations.access-ci.org/prepare-requests)
- [NVIDIA Academic Grant Program](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/industries/higher-education-research/academic-grant-program/)
- [AWS Cloud Credit for Research](https://aws.amazon.com/government-education/research-and-technical-computing/cloud-credit-for-research/)
- [GCP Research Credits — CU Boulder documentation](https://curc.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cloud/gcp/Google-Cloud-research-credits.html)
- [Modal for Academics](https://modal.com/academics)
- [Kaggle Pricing 2026](https://aisotools.com/pricing/kaggle)
- [Vast.ai Education](https://vast.ai/education)
- [RunPod Startup Program](https://www.runpod.io/startup-program)
- [Paperspace Advanced Technologies Group](https://www.paperspace.com/advanced-technologies-group)
- [CZI AI Computing GPU RFA](https://chanzuckerberg.com/rfa/ai-computing-gpu/)
- [Free Cloud GPU Credits 2026 — ThunderCompute](https://www.thundercompute.com/blog/free-cloud-gpu-credits)
